title: Regularity and Persistence in Non-Weinstein Liouville Geometry via Hyperbolic Dynamics
speaker: Surena Hozoori
abstract:
We explore the construction of non-Weinstein Liouville geometric objects based on Anosov 3-flows, introduced by Mitsumatsu, in the generalized framework of Liouville Interpolation Systems and non-singular partially hyperbolic flows. We discuss the subtle phenomena inherited from the regularity and persistence theory of hyperbolic dynamics in the resulting Liouville structures, and prove dynamical and geometric rigidity results in this context. Among other things, we show that Mitsumatsu's examples characterize 4-dimensional non-Weinstein Liouville geometry with 3-dimensional C1
-persistent transverse skeleton. Time permitting, we also draw applications to the regularity theory of the weak dominated bundles for non-singular partially hyperbolic 3-flows.

proof_stages.label: Liouville domains and the Weinstein/non-Weinstein dichotomy
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up Liouville forms, outward Liouville vector fields and contact-type boundary, then contrasts Weinstein domains, whose skeletons have at most half dimension, with non-Weinstein Liouville domains motivated by Mitsumatsu's Anosov-flow construction.

proof_stages.label: Three-dimensional Anosov dynamics and weak-foliation regularity
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines Anosov flows by the stable/unstable splitting, recalls classical and modern sources of examples, and emphasizes the low but subtle regularity of the invariant bundles and weak foliations.

proof_stages.label: Bicontact structures as a contact-geometric bridge
proof_stages.summary: Using positive and negative contact structures obtained from the bisectors of stable and unstable planes, the talk explains the Mitsumatsu--Eliashberg--Thurston observation and its converse formulation as projectively Anosov or dominated dynamics.

proof_stages.label: Liouville interpolation conditions for absolute hyperbolicity
proof_stages.summary: Linear interpolation between the two contact forms is analyzed on an interval times the three-manifold, showing that the Liouville condition along the crossing graph is equivalent to expansion or contraction of the induced norms and hence upgrades projective hyperbolicity to Anosov or partially hyperbolic behavior.

proof_stages.label: General Liouville interpolation systems and Floer-theoretic viewpoint
proof_stages.summary: The construction is enlarged to noncompact R x M Liouville interpolation systems with pointwise interpolation functions, encompassing the linear and exponential regimes and supporting homotopy-equivalence and Floer-theoretic invariants for Anosov-flow spaces.

proof_stages.label: Skeleton dynamics, synchronization, and regularity persistence
proof_stages.summary: The exact Lagrangian weak normal foliation forces the Liouville skeleton to be precisely the graph where the interpolation meets an invariant bundle, with synchronized Anosov dynamics on it, normal repellence, C1 persistence, uniqueness up to C1 conjugacy, and consequences for Hasselblatt-type regularity bounds.

proof_stages.label: Strict geometric rigidity via a sharpened Moser argument
proof_stages.summary: Because the Liouville vector field lies in the exact Lagrangian foliation and the Anosov direction can be fixed, a Moser argument recovers the Liouville form strictly, giving a one-to-one correspondence with dynamics up to conjugacy, smooth uniqueness of the Liouville flow, and equivalence of linear and exponential models up to compactness.

proof_stages.label: Intrinsic characterization by persistent three-dimensional skeletons
proof_stages.summary: The final theorem states that a Liouville manifold with an oriented C1-persistent three-dimensional skeleton transverse to the Liouville kernel has synchronized Anosov dynamics on the skeleton and is C1 strictly Liouville equivalent to a Mitsumatsu-type interpolation system, yielding an intrinsic correspondence with positive reparameterization classes of Anosov flows.
